_comment: Red-team validation PROCEDURES (not runnable offensive tooling) for the C2/exfil/impact seams. Each is an authorized-engagement methodology note: reference standard tooling, prove the trust seam, NON-DESTRUCTIVE success criterion, deconflict + share IOCs. language:text by design — these document how a red-teamer validates the seam without shipping a weapon.

C-c2.name: C2 egress-trust validation (authorized)
C-c2.authorization: Authorized engagement only; operator-controlled C2 infrastructure, in-scope hosts, agreed egress-test window, blue-team deconfliction.
C-c2.setup: Stand up a team server on in-scope operator infrastructure; agree the egress-test window and a deconfliction channel.
C-c2.script: Goal: show egress filtering trusts protocol conformance over destination intent. Using a standard red-team C2 framework on operator infra, establish a single beacon over a client-permitted protocol (HTTPS / DNS / CDN-fronted). Observe whether the callback survives the client's proxy and egress controls; record which channels succeed and the time-to-callback. Stop at the callback — do not stage further capability; this validates the egress-trust seam only.
C-c2.success_criterion: A beacon from an in-scope host reaches the operator team server through the client's egress controls (callback observed).
C-c2.cleanup: Tear down listeners and remove the beacon; hand the channel's IOCs to the blue team for detection tuning.
C-c2.safety: Authorized scope only; channel terminates at operator infrastructure; no destructive actions.

C2-malleable-beacon.name: Beacon-profile conformance validation
C2-malleable-beacon.authorization: Authorized engagement only; operator infra; agreed window; deconfliction.
C2-malleable-beacon.setup: Configure a single beacon with a profile shaped to look like permitted traffic, on operator infra.
C2-malleable-beacon.script: Validate that a sensor trusting traffic shape (not destination) admits a conformant beacon. Run one callback and record whether shape-based controls flag it. Report which profile characteristics passed so the blue team can tune. No payload staging beyond callback.
C2-malleable-beacon.success_criterion: A shape-conformant beacon callback is admitted by traffic-shape controls.
C2-malleable-beacon.cleanup: Remove beacon; share profile IOCs (JA3, headers, cadence) with defenders.
C2-malleable-beacon.safety: Authorized scope; single callback; no further capability.

C2-dns-tunnel.name: DNS-channel trust validation
C2-dns-tunnel.authorization: Authorized engagement only; operator-controlled authoritative name server for an in-scope test domain.
C2-dns-tunnel.setup: Operate an authoritative NS for an in-scope domain on operator infra; agree the test window.
C2-dns-tunnel.script: Validate that recursive DNS is trusted as benign egress. From an in-scope host, run a standard DNS-channel tool pointed at the operator NS and confirm a low-rate control channel resolves. Record whether the resolver path is monitored. Stop at channel-up; do not move data of value.
C2-dns-tunnel.success_criterion: A low-rate control channel resolves through the client's recursive resolver to the operator NS.
C2-dns-tunnel.cleanup: Tear down the NS responder; provide query-pattern IOCs (entropy, label length, volume).
C2-dns-tunnel.safety: Authorized scope; control-channel proof only; no sensitive data transited.

C2-icmp-covert.name: ICMP covert-path validation
C2-icmp-covert.authorization: Authorized engagement only; operator endpoint; agreed window.
C2-icmp-covert.setup: Operator-controlled responder on an in-scope segment.
C2-icmp-covert.script: Validate whether ICMP echo is trusted as harmless and permitted to egress. Confirm a minimal echo-payload control path reaches the operator responder. Record whether ICMP payload inspection exists. Proof-of-path only.
C2-icmp-covert.success_criterion: An echo-payload control message reaches the operator responder across the boundary.
C2-icmp-covert.safety: Authorized scope; path proof only; no data exfiltration.

C2-domain-fronting.name: Domain-fronting / CDN trust validation
C2-domain-fronting.authorization: Authorized engagement only; operator-controlled origin behind a CDN you are entitled to use; agreed window.
C2-domain-fronting.setup: Operator origin behind a CDN; an in-scope test host.
C2-domain-fronting.script: Validate that egress trusts the fronting/SNI hostname over the true origin. Establish a single callback where the visible SNI differs from the Host. Record whether the SNI/Host mismatch is detected. Callback only.
C2-domain-fronting.success_criterion: A callback succeeds with SNI ≠ Host through the client's egress.
C2-domain-fronting.cleanup: Remove the channel; share the SNI/Host-mismatch IOC.
C2-domain-fronting.safety: Authorized scope; single callback; no staging.

C2-saas-chat-channel.name: Trusted-SaaS channel validation
C2-saas-chat-channel.authorization: Authorized engagement only; operator-owned SaaS bot/workspace; agreed window.
C2-saas-chat-channel.setup: An operator-owned chat-platform bot/app and an in-scope host.
C2-saas-chat-channel.script: Validate that egress to a popular SaaS API is trusted as benign. From an in-scope host, confirm a low-rate control channel via the operator-owned bot API. Record whether SaaS-bound egress is baselined. Channel-up proof only.
C2-saas-chat-channel.success_criterion: A control channel via the trusted-SaaS API is admitted by egress controls.
C2-saas-chat-channel.cleanup: Disable the bot; report destination/volume IOCs.
C2-saas-chat-channel.safety: Authorized scope; operator-owned SaaS tenant; no sensitive data.

C2-redirector-profile.name: Redirector / JA3 trust validation
C2-redirector-profile.authorization: Authorized engagement only; operator redirector infra; agreed window.
C2-redirector-profile.setup: A redirector in front of the operator team server.
C2-redirector-profile.script: Validate that a sensor trusting client fingerprint/redirector reputation admits the traffic. Route one beacon through the redirector and record whether JA3/redirector reputation flags it. Proof only.
C2-redirector-profile.success_criterion: A beacon routed through the redirector is admitted without fingerprint-based blocking.
C2-redirector-profile.cleanup: Tear down redirector; share JA3/redirector IOCs.
C2-redirector-profile.safety: Authorized scope; single callback; no staging.

C2-saas-upload-exfil.name: Trusted-cloud-storage egress validation
C2-saas-upload-exfil.authorization: Authorized engagement only; operator-owned cloud-storage account; agreed window; synthetic canary data only.
C2-saas-upload-exfil.setup: Operator-owned storage account; a small CANARY file (no real client data).
C2-saas-upload-exfil.script: Validate that egress to trusted cloud storage is permitted. Upload a small synthetic canary from an in-scope host to the operator-owned account and confirm it is admitted. Use only the canary; never move real client data.
C2-saas-upload-exfil.success_criterion: A synthetic canary egresses to the trusted-storage destination without being blocked.
C2-saas-upload-exfil.cleanup: Delete the canary; report destination/volume IOCs.
C2-saas-upload-exfil.safety: Authorized scope; synthetic canary only; no real data exfiltrated.

C2-dns-exfil.name: DNS-exfil channel validation
C2-dns-exfil.authorization: Authorized engagement only; operator NS for an in-scope domain; synthetic canary; agreed window.
C2-dns-exfil.setup: Operator authoritative NS; a small canary token (no client data).
C2-dns-exfil.script: Validate that encoded-subdomain egress is trusted. Encode only a synthetic canary token into labels resolved at the operator NS; confirm reassembly. Never encode real data.
C2-dns-exfil.success_criterion: A synthetic canary reassembles at the operator NS from encoded queries.
C2-dns-exfil.cleanup: Tear down NS; report label-entropy/length IOCs.
C2-dns-exfil.safety: Authorized scope; canary only; no sensitive data.

C2-steganography-exfil.name: Steganographic-channel validation
C2-steganography-exfil.authorization: Authorized engagement only; synthetic canary; operator-controlled receiver; agreed window.
C2-steganography-exfil.setup: A benign sample image and a synthetic canary string.
C2-steganography-exfil.script: Validate that content-inspection trusts media as inert. Embed only a synthetic canary in a sample image and confirm it survives the egress path to an operator receiver. Never embed real data.
C2-steganography-exfil.success_criterion: A synthetic canary survives embedding + egress and is recovered by the operator receiver.
C2-steganography-exfil.cleanup: Delete sample; report stego-anomaly indicators for detection tuning.
C2-steganography-exfil.safety: Authorized scope; canary only; no real data.

C2-chunked-encrypted-exfil.name: Shaped-egress (DLP-evasion) validation
C2-chunked-encrypted-exfil.authorization: Authorized engagement only; synthetic canary; operator sink; agreed window.
C2-chunked-encrypted-exfil.setup: Operator HTTPS sink; a synthetic canary file.
C2-chunked-encrypted-exfil.script: Validate that DLP trusts declared content-type/shape. Transit only a chunked, encrypted synthetic canary at a low rate to the operator sink and confirm DLP does not flag it. Real data is never used.
C2-chunked-encrypted-exfil.success_criterion: A shaped synthetic canary transits without DLP alerting.

C2-removable-media-exfil.name: Removable-media egress validation
C2-removable-media-exfil.authorization: Authorized engagement only; operator-provided throwaway media; synthetic canary; on-site authorization.
C2-removable-media-exfil.setup: A throwaway USB volume and a synthetic canary file on an in-scope host.
C2-removable-media-exfil.script: Validate whether device-control trusts removable media. Copy only a synthetic canary to operator-provided media and record whether device-control/DLP blocks or logs it. No client data.
C2-removable-media-exfil.success_criterion: A synthetic canary is written to removable media without being blocked (or the gap in logging is shown).
C2-removable-media-exfil.cleanup: Wipe the media; report the USN/Event-4663 evidence to defenders.

C-exfil.name: Exfiltration-path trust validation
C-exfil.authorization: Authorized engagement only; synthetic canary; operator sink; agreed window.
C-exfil.setup: An operator-controlled sink and a synthetic canary.
C-exfil.script: Validate the generic egress-trust seam: move only a tagged synthetic canary over the channels the client permits and record which paths succeed. The deliverable is the list of permitted exfil paths + IOCs, not the movement of any real data.
C-exfil.success_criterion: A tagged synthetic canary reaches the operator sink over at least one permitted path.
C-exfil.cleanup: Delete canary; deliver path inventory + IOCs.
C-exfil.safety: Authorized scope; canary only; no real data.

C2-tier0-domain-admin.name: Tier-0 reach validation (non-destructive)
C2-tier0-domain-admin.authorization: Authorized engagement only; agreed Tier-0 objective; non-destructive proof-of-control.
C2-tier0-domain-admin.setup: Established foothold per the engagement; deconfliction for Tier-0 actions.
C2-tier0-domain-admin.script: Validate that the path to Tier-0/Domain-Admin control EXISTS without exercising destructive control. Use read-only checks (e.g., confirm replication rights / group membership / ticket-forging capability via a benign verification) to demonstrate reach. Stop at proof-of-control; do not perform estate-wide changes.
C2-tier0-domain-admin.success_criterion: Read-only evidence that Tier-0 control is reachable (e.g., DCSync-capable rights confirmed) without exercising it.
C2-tier0-domain-admin.cleanup: Document the path; share the precise privilege chain with defenders.
C2-tier0-domain-admin.safety: Authorized scope; proof-of-reach only; no destructive or estate-wide action.

C2-crown-jewel-access.name: Crown-jewel reachability validation (non-destructive)
C2-crown-jewel-access.authorization: Authorized engagement only; agreed crown-jewel target; minimal-access proof.
C2-crown-jewel-access.setup: Foothold with a path toward the sensitive store, per scope.
C2-crown-jewel-access.script: Validate that an authenticated position can reach the crown-jewel store. Demonstrate read reachability of a single agreed canary record (or metadata) — never bulk-stage or exfiltrate real sensitive data. The proof is reachability, not collection.
C2-crown-jewel-access.success_criterion: Reachability of an agreed canary record/metadata is demonstrated without bulk access.
C2-crown-jewel-access.cleanup: Document the access path; recommend segmentation/authz fixes.
C2-crown-jewel-access.safety: Authorized scope; single canary; no bulk access or exfiltration.

C-impact.name: Impact-capability validation (non-destructive)
C-impact.authorization: Authorized engagement only; explicit written sign-off; non-destructive demonstration.
C-impact.setup: Foothold with the privilege the destructive action would require.
C-impact.script: Validate that the position is trusted to perform a destructive/disruptive action WITHOUT performing it. Confirm the capability via a read-only permission check or a dry-run flag against a throwaway lab object. Never encrypt, wipe, deface, or disrupt production. The seam is authorization-of-position vs authorization-of-act.
C-impact.success_criterion: A permission/dry-run check confirms the destructive capability exists, with no destructive effect.
C-impact.cleanup: Remove the throwaway object; report the over-broad authorization.
C-impact.safety: Authorized scope; capability proof only; no destruction.

C2-business-process-disruption.name: Availability-impact capability validation (non-destructive)
C2-business-process-disruption.authorization: Authorized engagement only; written sign-off; no production disruption.
C2-business-process-disruption.setup: Access to the business process's control surface in a lab/staging mirror if available.
C2-business-process-disruption.script: Validate that an authenticated principal could disrupt a business process, without disrupting it. Demonstrate the capability via a permission check or a staging/lab dry-run only. Production availability is never affected.
C2-business-process-disruption.success_criterion: Capability to disrupt is shown via permission/dry-run in lab/staging, with production untouched.
C2-business-process-disruption.cleanup: Reset staging; report the authorization gap.
C2-business-process-disruption.safety: Authorized scope; lab/staging or permission-check only; no production impact.

C2-fraudulent-transaction.name: Transaction-authorization seam validation (non-destructive)
C2-fraudulent-transaction.authorization: Authorized engagement only; written sign-off; no real transaction.
C2-fraudulent-transaction.setup: Access to the payment workflow in a sandbox/UAT environment.
C2-fraudulent-transaction.script: Validate that the workflow trusts an authenticated position to move funds, without moving real funds. Demonstrate reachability of the transaction-initiation step in a sandbox/UAT environment or via a permission check. Never initiate a real transfer.
C2-fraudulent-transaction.success_criterion: Reachability of the funds-movement step is shown in sandbox/UAT (or via permission check), with no real transaction.
C2-fraudulent-transaction.cleanup: Reset sandbox; report the authorization-of-position gap.
C2-fraudulent-transaction.safety: Authorized scope; sandbox/UAT or permission-check only; no real funds moved.
